articles.title: HyperFrames: Write HTML and JavaScript to Create Videos
articles.description: An open-source framework for creating and rendering videos with HTML and JavaScript. Essentially a simpler non-React alternative to Remotion. It includes a variety of built-in blocks/components for common video effects and elements, and can also composite existing video and audio clips. GitHub repo.
articles.author: HeyGen

articles.title: The Vercel Breach That Started with a Roblox Cheat
articles.description: An employee of an AI tool provider used by a Vercel employee was compromised by malware (bundled with a Roblox cheat!) and the attacker used that foothold, by way of Google Workspace, to reach a subset of Vercel customers’ environment variables.
articles.author: Vercel

articles.title: ▶  Evan You's State of Vue 2026 Talk
articles.description: A month ago, Evan You (of Vue.js and VoidZero fame) gave his annual address. Less Vue-focused than usual (though Vapor Mode is “almost ready”), the talk focuses on Vite-ecosystem updates covering Vite 8, Vite+, and Void.
articles.author: Evan You / Vue.js Amsterdam

articles.title: How I Resolved 15K Circular Dependencies
articles.description: A senior Microsoft engineer’s retrospective of clearing ~15,000 project-level circular dependencies from a 7 million line(!) TypeScript monorepo, with reusable ideas for anyone wrangling a large TS workspace.
articles.author: Stefan Haas

articles.title: The Vertical Codebase
articles.description: Structuring an app with folders like components/, hooks/, and utils/ feels tidy at first, but gets harder to live with over time. Dominik makes the case for a vertical, domain-first approach.
articles.author: Dominik Dorfmeister

articles.title: 🔒 The OWASP NPM Security Best Practices Cheat Sheet
articles.description: A useful, long-standing checklist that continues to be updated with recent updates tackling disabling lifecycle scripts, typosquatting, trusted publishing, and dependency confusion.
articles.author: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series

articles.title: How We Made the Angular Compiler Faster Using AI
articles.description: Two of VoidZero’s developers wanted to see how fast an Angular compiler they could make. Very fast, it turns out.
articles.author: Brooklyn and Michael Dong (VoidZero)

articles.title: Bun v1.3.13: Smarter Testing, Streaming Installs, and Less Memory
articles.description: The Bun runtime has had a great run of releases, including last week’s v1.3.12 with built-in browser automation. Now, bun test gets numerous enhancements with --isolate, --parallel, --shard and --changed options for test env isolation, parallelization, and to run only test files affected by recent changes. The runtime now uses 5% less memory, bun install gets faster, and more.
articles.author: Jarred Sumner

articles.title: Animata: Over 100 Animated React Components
articles.description: A suite of novel animation-focused React components you don't often see elsewhere, including animated beams, spreading cards, and a Slack-style intro screen.
articles.author: Codse

articles.title: 📄 officeParser: A Library to Parse Common Office-Related Formats
articles.description: Work with formats like docx, pptx, xlsx, odt and others used by office suites, both in the browser and server-side. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Harsh Ankur

articles.title: 🎵 tiks: Procedural UI Sounds for the Web
articles.description: Clicks, pops and pings synthesized with the Web Audio API (so it’s tiny).
articles.author: Rexa

articles.description: Node.js is moving to support the Temporal API by default, most likely in Node v26 which is expected next week.
Salesforce ecosystem devs are used to using specific frameworks within its walled garden; now you can build native React apps on Salesforce.
Rust's official package/crate registry, crates.io, is migrating from Ember.js to Svelte 5.
Node.js 24.15.0 (LTS) – require(esm) and the module compile cache are marked as stable, and --max-heap-size has been added.
Fable 5.0 – A mature F# transpiler that targets JavaScript (plus other languages). v5.0 adds .NET 10 and F# 10 support.
uuid 14.0 – Create RFC9562-compliant UUIDs (v1 through v7).

articles.title: Building a UI Without Breakpoints
articles.description: Amit makes the case for moving away from global viewport breakpoints, instead shifting to using modern CSS such as clamp(), container queries, and auto-fit to create fluid interfaces no matter the screen.
articles.author: Amit Sheen

articles.title: font-family Doesn’t Fall Back the Way You Think
articles.description: Harry clarifies how inheritance actually works when loading in font stacks, provides some welcome nuance on why we often see flashes of unexpected text or even layout shifts, and of course shares how to easily fix things.
articles.author: Harry Roberts

articles.title: Firefox 150: Release Notes for Developers
articles.description: Enhancements include light-dark() image support, changes to the CSS color-mix() function (with it now accepting multiple color values), the addition of media-based pseudo-classes, and more.
articles.author: MDN

articles.title: Delivering a Dynamic Hexagonal World Map in 10KB
articles.description: A walkthrough of building a hexagon-based SVG world map with the whole thing weighing in at just 10KB. Covers simplifying high-resolution GeoJSON with Turf.js and projecting it with d3-geo.
articles.author: Ben Schwarz

articles.title: First Experiments with the New 'HTML in Canvas' API
articles.description: A fun primer looking at what we’ll be able to do with the HTML-in-Canvas API. It’s currently experimental, and is only available behind a flag — but these demos do a good job of showing what sort of things it will unlock — ”not just a new API; it is a new workflow mindset”.
articles.author: Amit Sheen

articles.title: Details That Make Interfaces Feel Better
articles.description: Plenty of good ‘small-detail’ UI advice here, and the learnings are also available bundled up as an AI skill.
articles.author: Jakub Krehel

articles.title: Making Your Site Visible to LLMs: 6 Techniques That Work, 8 That Don't
articles.description: There aren’t any particularly accepted standards today, but these techniques are easy enough to put in place if you do want LLMs to return your sites in results.
articles.author: Irina Nazarova (Evil Martians)

articles.title: Why Some Images Look Brighter Than Your Screen
articles.description: A technical exploration of why some images can sometimes seem brighter than a surrounding user interface when viewed on certain modern displays. Tom talks HDR, color profiles, gain maps, and more, sharing how such techniques can be utilised. There’s also a tool here to generate such images. (I opened this on my iPhone, and true enough, the image really does pop.)
articles.author: Tom Nick

articles.title: Green Components: How Your Design System Can Aid Sustainability Goals
articles.description: Tips on how to make your site more sustainable via your design system, and measure the effects of your efforts.
articles.author: Hidde De Vries

articles.title: ✉️ MJML 5.0: The Responsive Email Framework
articles.description: HTML emails are a headache to code (trust us!) but MJML provides a component-based framework to make it less daunting if you don’t read CanIEmail on a regular basis. There's an upgrade guide to v5.0 if you're an existing user.
articles.author: Mailjet

articles.title: 🔊 tiks: Procedural UI Sounds for the Web
articles.description: Bleeps, blips, and clicks synthesized with the Web Audio API (so it’s tiny).
articles.author: Rexa

articles.title: HyperFrames: Write HTML & JavaScript to Create Videos
articles.description: An open-source framework for creating and rendering videos. Includes a variety of built-in blocks/components for common video effects and elements, and can also composite existing video and audio clips. GitHub repo.
articles.author: HeyGen

articles.title: µCSS: A Lightweight, Themeable CSS Framework
articles.description: Includes default class-less styles in 20 different themes, dark mode toggle, 36 pre-built components, a mobile-first grid, and templating in PHP.
articles.author: Digicreon

articles.title: Formisch: A Modular, Type-Safe Form Library for Any Framework
articles.description: A headless solution boasting a small bundle size with a simple, intuitive API to manage form state, handle user input, and Valibot schema-based validation. Try out some demos in the playground on the docs site.
articles.author: Open Circle

articles.title: TypeGPU: A Type-Safe WebGPU Toolkit
articles.description: A WebGPU toolkit with advanced type inference and the ability to write shaders in TypeScript. Here’s a live example (flip the toggle to see the source).
articles.author: Software Mansion

articles.title: 🦋 bsky-comments: A Zero-Dependency Web Component to Embed Bluesky Comments
articles.description: No dependencies, works with any framework, it’s easy to style with CSS (i.e., no tricky Shadow DOM), and supports full threads with nested replies.
articles.author: Florian Schepp

articles.title: Liquid Glass: WebGL Glass Effects for HTML Elements
articles.description: A fun WebGL-powered effect you can apply, by way of JavaScript, to the HTML elements of your choice to reproduce realistic glass refraction and blur effects a la iOS 26. And, yes, there's an interactive playground (shown above).
articles.author: Yassine Bouanane
